item.title: [Daily] Skills.sh snapshot (2026-02-28)
item.description: Daily snapshot. Providers: skills.sh All Time skills: 40952 Trending skills: 19672 Hot skills: 1979 Top All Time: find-skills (356910) Top Trending: agent-browser (16611) Top Hot: brief (89)

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#11): video-prompting-guide
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #11 Installs: 63 Source: inference-sh-9/skills Description: Best practices and techniques for writing effective AI video generation prompts. Covers: Veo, Seedance, Wan, Grok, Kling, Runway, Pika, Sora prompting strategies. Learn: shot types, camera movements, lighting, pacing, style keywords, negative prompts. Use for: improving video quality, getting consistent results, professional video prompts. Triggers: video prompt, how to prompt video, veo prompts, video generation tips, better ai video, video prompt engineering, video prompt guide, video prompt template, ai video tips, video prompt best practices, video prompt examples, cinematography prompts

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#12): chat-ui
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #12 Installs: 118 Source: inference-sh-9/skills Description: name: chat-ui

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#13): ai-voice-cloning
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #13 Installs: 63 Source: inference-sh-9/skills Description: AI voice generation, text-to-speech, and voice synthesis via inference.sh CLI. Models: Kokoro TTS, DIA, Chatterbox, Higgs, VibeVoice for natural speech. Capabilities: multiple voices, emotions, accents, long-form narration, conversation. Use for: voiceovers, audiobooks, podcasts, video narration, accessibility. Triggers: voice cloning, tts, text to speech, ai voice, voice generation, voice synthesis, voice over, narration, speech synthesis, ai narrator, elevenlabs alternative, natural voice, realistic speech, voice ai

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#15): newsletter-curation
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #15 Installs: 77 Source: inference-sh-9/skills Description: Newsletter curation with content sourcing, editorial structure, and subscriber growth strategies. Covers issue formatting, link roundups, commentary style, and sending cadence. Use for: email newsletters, link roundups, weekly digests, curated content, creator newsletters. Triggers: newsletter, email newsletter, newsletter curation, weekly digest, link roundup, curated newsletter, newsletter writing, newsletter format, subscriber growth, newsletter strategy, content curation, newsletter template

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#16): vercel-react-native-skills
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #16 Installs: 47 Source: vercel-labs/agent-skills Description: React Native and Expo best practices for building performant mobile apps. Use when building React Native components, optimizing list performance, implementing animations, or working with native modules. Triggers on tasks involving React Native, Expo, mobile performance, or native platform APIs.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#17): context7
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #17 Installs: 39 Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Fetch up-to-date library documentation via Context7 API. Use PROACTIVELY when: (1) Working with ANY external library (React, Next.js, Supabase, etc.) (2) User asks about library APIs, patterns, or best practices (3) Implementing features that rely on third-party packages (4) Debugging library-specific issues (5) Need current documentation beyond training data cutoff (6) AND MOST IMPORTANTLY, when you are installing dependencies, libraries, or frameworks you should ALWAYS check the docs to see what the latest versions are. Do not rely on outdated knowledge. Always prefer this over guessing library APIs or using outdated knowledge.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#18): competitor-teardown
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #18 Installs: 63 Source: inference-sh-9/skills Description: Structured competitive analysis with feature matrices, SWOT, positioning maps, and UX review. Covers research frameworks, pricing comparison, review mining, and visual deliverables. Use for: market research, competitive intelligence, investor decks, product strategy, sales enablement. Triggers: competitor analysis, competitive analysis, competitor teardown, market research, competitive intelligence, swot analysis, competitor comparison, market landscape, competitor review, competitive landscape, feature comparison, market positioning

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#19): vet
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #19 Installs: 39 Source: imbue-ai/vet Description: Run vet immediately after ANY logical unit of code changes. Do not batch your changes, do not wait to be asked to run vet, make sure you are proactive.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#20): planner
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #20 Installs: 38 Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Create comprehensive, phased implementation plans with sprints and atomic tasks. Use when user says: "make a plan", "create a plan", "plan this out", "plan the implementation", "help me plan", "design a plan", "draft a plan", "write a plan", "outline the steps", "break this down into tasks", "what's the plan for", or any similar planning request. Also triggers on explicit "/planner" or "/plan" commands.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#22): talking-head-production
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #22 Installs: 68 Source: inference-sh-9/skills Description: Talking head video production with AI avatars, lipsync, and voiceover. Covers portrait requirements, audio quality, OmniHuman, PixVerse lipsync, Dia TTS. Use for: spokesperson videos, course content, social media, presentations, demos. Triggers: talking head, avatar video, lipsync, lip sync, ai spokesperson, virtual presenter, ai presenter, omnihuman, talking avatar, video presenter, ai talking head, presenter video, ai face video

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#24): product-changelog
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #24 Installs: 62 Source: inference-sh-9/skills Description: Product changelog and release notes that users actually read. Covers categorization, user-facing language, visuals, and distribution. Use for: release notes, changelogs, product updates, feature announcements, versioning. Triggers: changelog, release notes, product update, version notes, what's new, feature announcement, product changelog, update log, release announcement, version release, product release, ship notes

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#25): related-skill
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #25 Installs: 62 Source: inference-sh-9/skills Description: Discover and install related skills from inference.sh skill registry. Helps find complementary skills for your AI workflow. Use for: skill discovery, workflow expansion, capability exploration. Triggers: related skills, find skills, skill discovery, complementary skills, expand workflow, more capabilities, similar skills, skill suggestions

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#26): read-github
item.description:
New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #26 Installs: 37 Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Read and search GitHub repository documentation via gitmcp.io MCP service.
**WHEN TO USE:** - User provides a GitHub URL - User mentions a specific repo in owner/repo format - User asks "what does this repo do?", "read the docs for X repo", or similar - User wants to search code or docs within a repo

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#27): gemini-computer-use
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #27 Installs: 37 Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Build and run Gemini 2.5 Computer Use browser-control agents with Playwright. Use when a user wants to automate web browser tasks via the Gemini Computer Use model, needs an agent loop (screenshot → function_call → action → function_response), or asks to integrate safety confirmation for risky UI actions.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#28): openai-docs-skill
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #28 Installs: 37 Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Query the OpenAI developer documentation via the OpenAI Docs MCP server using CLI (curl/jq). Use whenever a task involves the OpenAI API (Responses, Chat Completions, Realtime, etc.), OpenAI SDKs, ChatGPT Apps SDK, Codex, MCP integrations, endpoint schemas, parameters, limits, or migrations and you need up-to-date official guidance.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#29): swarm-planner
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #29 Installs: 37 Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: [EXPLICIT INVOCATION ONLY] Creates dependency-aware implementation plans optimized for parallel multi-agent execution.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#30): plan-harder
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #30 Installs: 37 Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Use when user specfically says 'plan harder'.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#31): llm-council
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #31 Installs: 37 Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Orchestrate a configurable, multi-member CLI planning council (Codex, Claude Code, Gemini, OpenCode, or custom) to produce independent implementation plans, anonymize and randomize them, then judge and merge into one final plan. Use when you need a robust, bias-resistant planning workflow, structured JSON outputs, retries, and failure handling across multiple CLI agents.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#32): agent browser
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #32 Installs: 37 Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: A fast Rust-based headless browser automation CLI with Node.js fallback that enables AI agents to navigate, click, type, and snapshot pages via structured commands.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#33): super-swarm-spark
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #33 Installs: 37 Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Only to be triggered by explicit super-swarm-spark commands.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#34): parallel-task
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #34 Installs: 37 Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Execute plan files by launching multiple parallel subagents to complete tasks simultaneously. Triggers on explicit "/parallel-task" commands.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#35): parallel-task-spark
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #35 Installs: 37 Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Only to be triggered by explicit /parallel-task-spark commands.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#36): role-creator
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #36 Installs: 37 Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Create and install Codex custom agent roles in ~/.codex/config.toml, generate role config files, enforce supported keys, and guide users through required role inputs (model, reasoning effort, developer_instructions).

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#37): codex-subagent
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #37 Installs: 37 Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Spawn Codex subagents via background shell to offload context-heavy work. Use for: deep research (3+ searches), codebase exploration (8+ files), multi-step workflows, exploratory tasks, long-running operations, documentation generation, or any other task where the intermediate steps will use large numbers of tokens.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#38): markdown-url
item.description:
New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #38 Installs: 37 Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Route any website you need to visit through markdown.new by prefixing the URL.
**WHEN TO USE:** - You would normally open a website link to read content (docs, blog posts, changelogs, GitHub issues, etc.) - You need a cleaner, Markdown-friendly view for copying notes or summarizing

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#39): frontend-design
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #39 Installs: 37 Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#41): nodejs-backend-patterns
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #41 Installs: 15 Source: wshobson/agents Description: Build production-ready Node.js backend services with Express/Fastify, implementing middleware patterns, error handling, authentication, database integration, and API design best practices. Use when creating Node.js servers, REST APIs, GraphQL backends, or microservices architectures.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#42): search
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #42 Installs: 9 Source: tavily-ai/skills Description: Search the web using Tavily's LLM-optimized search API. Returns relevant results with content snippets, scores, and metadata. Use when you need to find web content on any topic without writing code.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#43): vercel-react-best-practices
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #43 Installs: 37 Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: React and Next.js performance optimization guidelines from Vercel Engineering. This skill should be used when writing, reviewing, or refactoring React/Next.js code to ensure optimal performance patterns. Triggers on tasks involving React components, Next.js pages, data fetching, bundle optimization, or performance improvements.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#45): github-actions-templates
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #45 Installs: 10 Source: wshobson/agents Description: Create production-ready GitHub Actions workflows for automated testing, building, and deploying applications. Use when setting up CI/CD with GitHub Actions, automating development workflows, or creating reusable workflow templates.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#46): python-executor
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #46 Installs: 116 Source: inference-sh-9/skills Description: Execute Python code in a safe sandboxed environment via [inference.sh](https://inference.sh). Pre-installed: NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, requests, BeautifulSoup, Selenium, Playwright, MoviePy, Pillow, OpenCV, trimesh, and 100+ more libraries. Use for: data processing, web scraping, image manipulation, video creation, 3D model processing, PDF generation, API calls, automation scripts. Triggers: python, execute code, run script, web scraping, data analysis, image processing, video editing, 3D models, automation, pandas, matplotlib

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#47): press-release-writing
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #47 Installs: 55 Source: inference-sh-9/skills Description: Press release writing in AP style with inverted pyramid structure. Covers formatting, datelines, quotes, boilerplates, and fact-checking. Use for: product launches, funding announcements, partnerships, company news, events. Triggers: press release, pr writing, media release, news release, announcement, product launch announcement, funding announcement, company news, media advisory, ap style, press statement, news wire

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#49): error-handling-patterns
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #49 Installs: 11 Source: wshobson/agents Description: Master error handling patterns across languages including exceptions, Result types, error propagation, and graceful degradation to build resilient applications. Use when implementing error handling, designing APIs, or improving application reliability.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#50): landing-page-design
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #50 Installs: 65 Source: inference-sh-9/skills Description: Landing page conversion optimization with layout rules, hero section design, and CTA psychology. Covers above-the-fold formula, social proof placement, mobile design, and F-pattern reading. Use for: startup landing pages, product pages, SaaS marketing, conversion optimization. Triggers: landing page, hero section, above the fold, conversion optimization, landing page design, cta button, hero image, landing page layout, saas landing page, product page design, conversion rate, landing page best practices

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#6): remotion-render
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #6 Installs: 891 Source: inference-sh-9/skills Description: Render videos from React/Remotion component code via inference.sh. Pass TSX code, get MP4. Supports all Remotion APIs: useCurrentFrame, useVideoConfig, spring, interpolate, AbsoluteFill, Sequence. Configurable resolution, FPS, duration, codec. Use for: programmatic video generation, animated graphics, motion design, data-driven videos, React animations to video. Triggers: remotion, render video from code, tsx to video, react video, programmatic video, remotion render, code to video, animated video, motion graphics code, react animation video

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#7): nano-banana-2
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #7 Installs: 900 Source: inference-sh-9/skills Description: Generate images with Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview (Nano Banana 2) via inference.sh CLI. Capabilities: text-to-image, image editing, multi-image input (up to 14 images), Google Search grounding. Triggers: nano banana 2, nanobanana 2, gemini 3.1 flash image, gemini 3 1 flash image preview, google image generation

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#8): twitter-thread-creation
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #8 Installs: 69 Source: inference-sh-9/skills Description: Twitter/X thread writing with hook tweets, thread structure, and engagement optimization. Covers tweet formatting, character limits, media attachments, and posting strategies. Use for: Twitter threads, X posts, tweet storms, Twitter content, social media writing. Triggers: twitter thread, tweet thread, x thread, twitter post, tweet writing, thread creation, tweet storm, twitter content, x post, twitter writing, twitter hook, tweet formatting, thread structure

item.title: [Trending] New entry (#30): ai-pdf-filler-cli
item.description: New entry in Trending leaderboard. Rank: #30 Installs: 3144 Source: askyourpdf/ai-pdf-filler Description: Use simplicity-cli to autofill PDF forms from local files or URLs, monitor async task execution, and download filled PDFs. Use when a user asks to fill forms with AI, run form autofill from context/source documents, check task status, wait for completion, or debug CLI workflow/auth behavior.

item.title: [Trending] New entry (#34): agent-email-cli
item.description: New entry in Trending leaderboard. Rank: #34 Installs: 2625 Source: zaddy6/agent-email-skill Description: Operate the agent-email CLI to create disposable inboxes, poll for new mail, retrieve full message details, and manage local mailbox profiles. Use when the user needs terminal-based email inbox access for LLM or agent automation workflows.

item.title: [Trending] New entry (#43): brief
item.description: New entry in Trending leaderboard. Rank: #43 Installs: 2003 Source: calm-north/seojuice-skills Description: Produce an editor-ready content brief for a target keyword or topic. Use when the user asks for a content brief, writing guidelines, article outline, SEO brief, what to include in a blog post, or how to structure content for a keyword. For choosing which topics to write about, see find-keywords. For a full site-wide SEO audit, see audit.

item.title: [Trending] New entry (#44): audit
item.description: New entry in Trending leaderboard. Rank: #44 Installs: 2002 Source: calm-north/seojuice-skills Description: Run a comprehensive SEO audit on a website covering technical health, on-page optimization, content quality, and backlink profile. Use when the user asks for an SEO audit, site review, SEO health check, "what's wrong with my SEO", website analysis, or a full diagnostic of their site's search performance. For speed-specific issues, see audit-speed. For technical crawl/index issues only, see diagnose-seo.

item.title: [Trending] New entry (#45): optimize-for-ai
item.description: New entry in Trending leaderboard. Rank: #45 Installs: 1996 Source: calm-north/seojuice-skills Description: Optimize content for AI search engines — ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, Gemini, Google AI Overviews. Use when the user asks about AI SEO, AISO, getting cited by AI, appearing in AI answers, answer engine optimization, AEO, GEO, LLMO, AI Overviews, zero-click search, or how to appear in ChatGPT/Perplexity results. For traditional SEO, see diagnose-seo.

item.title: [Trending] New entry (#46): build-links
item.description: New entry in Trending leaderboard. Rank: #46 Installs: 1995 Source: calm-north/seojuice-skills Description: Design a link building campaign with prospect identification, asset creation, and outreach strategy. Use when the user asks about link building, backlinks, domain authority, how to get backlinks, outreach campaigns, digital PR, guest posting, or link acquisition. For internal linking, see fix-linking.

item.title: [Trending] New entry (#47): find-keywords
item.description: New entry in Trending leaderboard. Rank: #47 Installs: 1982 Source: calm-north/seojuice-skills Description: Build a prioritized keyword list for a website, topic, or campaign. Use when the user asks about keyword research, finding keywords to target, building a keyword list, search demand, keyword difficulty, intent mapping, or which keywords to prioritize. For writing content around keywords, see brief. For grouping keywords into clusters, see build-clusters.

item.title: [All Time] Rank up (11 → 10): azure-deploy
item.description: All Time leaderboard rank change. Previous rank: #11 Current rank: #10 Delta: +1 Installs: 88530 (prev 86371) Source: microsoft/github-copilot-for-azure Description: Execute deployment to Azure. Final step after preparation and validation. Runs azd up, azd deploy, or infrastructure provisioning commands. USE FOR: run azd up, run azd deploy, execute deployment, provision infrastructure, push to production, go live, ship it, deploy web app, deploy container app, deploy static site, deploy Azure Functions, bicep deploy, terraform apply. DO NOT USE FOR: creating or building apps (use azure-prepare), validating before deploy (use azure-validate).

item.title: [Hot] Rank down (10 → 14): diagnose-seo
item.description: Hot leaderboard rank change. Previous rank: #10 Current rank: #14 Delta: -4 Installs: 30 (prev 58) Source: calm-north/seojuice-skills Description: Structured diagnostic for technical SEO problems. Use when the user asks about crawl issues, indexation problems, why pages aren't being indexed, robots.txt questions, canonical errors, sitemap issues, rendering problems, or general technical SEO troubleshooting. For Core Web Vitals and page speed, see audit-speed.

item.title: [Hot] Rank down (11 → 48): fix-linking
item.description: Hot leaderboard rank change. Previous rank: #11 Current rank: #48 Delta: -37 Installs: 24 (prev 58) Source: calm-north/seojuice-skills Description: Design or audit internal link structure for a website. Use when the user asks about internal linking, link architecture, orphan pages, PageRank flow, anchor text, site structure, silo architecture, or why pages aren't ranking despite good content. For external link building, see build-links.

item.title: [Hot] Rank down (12 → 40): rank-local
item.description: Hot leaderboard rank change. Previous rank: #12 Current rank: #40 Delta: -28 Installs: 26 (prev 57) Source: calm-north/seojuice-skills Description: Build a local SEO strategy covering Google Business Profile, local citations, reviews, and NAP consistency. Use when the user asks about local SEO, Google Business Profile, Google Maps ranking, local pack, NAP consistency, local citations, review strategy, or ranking for "[service] near me" searches.

item.title: [Hot] Rank down (13 → 44): build-clusters
item.description: Hot leaderboard rank change. Previous rank: #13 Current rank: #44 Delta: -31 Installs: 26 (prev 57) Source: calm-north/seojuice-skills Description: Build a topical authority cluster from a seed keyword or topic. Use when the user asks about topic clusters, content silos, pillar pages, topical authority, content architecture, or how to structure content around a theme. For finding the right keywords, see find-keywords. For writing individual pieces, see brief.

item.title: [Hot] Rank down (2 → 5): optimize-for-ai
item.description: Hot leaderboard rank change. Previous rank: #2 Current rank: #5 Delta: -3 Installs: 73 (prev 164) Source: calm-north/seojuice-skills Description: Optimize content for AI search engines — ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, Gemini, Google AI Overviews. Use when the user asks about AI SEO, AISO, getting cited by AI, appearing in AI answers, answer engine optimization, AEO, GEO, LLMO, AI Overviews, zero-click search, or how to appear in ChatGPT/Perplexity results. For traditional SEO, see diagnose-seo.
